title: iberis spathulata subsp. nana
intro: Iberis spathulata subsp. nana, also known as Dwarf Candytuft, is a low-growing, flowering plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory, which makes it useful in treating conditions such as arthritis and urinary tract infections. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Dwarf Candytuft is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety in these applications.
